description: Paginated index of VGA (Visual GUI Automation) runs. Shows one row per row of runner.vga_runs joined to runner.vga_state_machines on state_machine_id. Columns: truncated run id linking to /vga/runs/[runId], state machine name, target process, status badge, started-at timestamp, duration. Supports filtering by status, target_process (exact match), and started_at date range (since/until). Filter state is mirrored into the URL query string so a reload or shared link restores the view. Pagination: 50 rows/page; total row count is computed server-side via COUNT(*) OVER() in the same query as the page slice. This page is the user's window into the Python VGA worker's execution history — the canonical place to see which runs succeeded, failed, drifted, or are still running for a given app.

groups.description: Top-level layout: a sticky header with back-to-/vga link, heading, and total badge; a scrollable main area with a filters card followed by the runs table card. The viewport height is clamped to `calc(100vh-44px)` so the inner main section scrolls independently of the app shell.
groups.name: Page Structure & Layout

groups.description: The filters card exposes four filters: status (Select), target_process (free-text Input with exact-match semantics), since (date Input), until (date Input), plus a Reset button. Changing any filter resets page to 1 and triggers a fresh /api/vga/runs query.
groups.name: Filters Card

groups.description: When runs exist, a 6-column table renders the current page of results: Run id (truncated to first 8 chars, linked to /vga/runs/[runId]), State machine name, Target process, Status badge, Started-at (locale datetime), Duration. Rows are ordered by started_at DESC.
groups.name: Runs Table

groups.description: Each row's Status cell renders a Badge whose variant encodes status. The statusTone helper maps: succeeded|success→secondary, failed→destructive, drifted→outline, running→default, any other→outline. The label shown capitalizes the first letter except for unknown statuses which pass through raw.
groups.name: Run Status Badges

groups.description: The four filters plus the page index are mirrored into the URL query string. A shared link or bookmark must reproduce the exact filtered view. Sync is bidirectional via a single `lastSyncedRef` to prevent the state→URL effect from stomping params the user navigated in with.
groups.name: URL ↔ Filter State Sync

groups.description: The page is backed by the Next.js route handler at /api/vga/runs which executes a single SELECT over runner.vga_runs LEFT JOIN runner.vga_state_machines. The contract is observable from the page: the table's column values come directly from the row shape, totals come from COUNT(*) OVER(), filter params shape the WHERE clause.
groups.name: GET /api/vga/runs contract
